zotero.abstractNote: We consider the problem of quantifying the worst-case risk of a system under distributional ambiguity. The ambiguity is represented by a set of probability distributions that satisfy a set of moment constraints. We propose a novel approach to solve this problem by embedding the distributions into a reproducing kernel Hilbert space (RKHS). The moment constraints are then expressed as linear constraints on the kernel mean embeddings. This formulation leads to a convex optimization problem, which can be solved efficiently. We provide theoretical guarantees for the convergence of the proposed estimator and demonstrate its effectiveness on several numerical examples.
